raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX I attended an informational video call with XXXX XXXX for mentoring services. At the end of the call, I signed an agreement for 3 months of mentoring services and paid {$3500.00}. We started mentoring services on XX/XX/XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted XXXX XXXX via XXXX and asked if we can pause services due to a medical issue I was experiencing. We agreed to tentatively resume on Wednesday XX/XX/XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I realized that my medical condition was not improving as quickly as I would like, so decided that I would like to cancel the remainder of our service contract and receive a pro-rated refund. So I sent XXXX a message on XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX. He replied on XX/XX/XXXX and encouraged me not to give up and insisted that he would like to continue mentoring me. I advised him that I would like to stop services and would like to focus on recovery, and asked again for a prorated refund. However, I never heard back. 

In the Termination clause of the agreement I signed, it states If You terminate the Agreement before the Services have been completed, You will be liable to Us for a pro rata share of the total cost of the Services as outlined in Section XXXX. '' 3 months ( XXXX weeks ) of mentoring services is 85 days ( if you include the first and the last day ). I received mentoring services starting on XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX. That period of time is 43 days ( inclusive ) of services rendered. That leaves 42 days left of mentoring that I am refusing service on and for which I submitted a dispute to American Express on XX/XX/XXXX to request a credit from American Express in the amount of XXXX : ( XX/XX/XXXX ) * {$3500.00} = {$1700.00}. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, American Express closed the dispute without reading my statement or carefully looking at the documentation I provided, if at all. So I re-opened the dispute on that same day. However, the dispute was closed again on XX/XX/XXXX with the vendor citing my lack of written notice to terminate the contract ( and again, American Express failing to read through the documentation I provided ). However, I did in fact provide written notice to XXXX XXXX via XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX ( this was mentioned in the dispute and the re-opening of the dispute, but was ignored both times ). The dispute itself should also serve as written notice.
